task_completion.description: Open the pUC19.gb file in UGENE
task_completion.visual_evidence: The UGENE application is open and displays the pUC19 sequence or plasmid data.

task_completion.description: Annotate the sequence with EcoRI, BamHI, and HindIII restriction sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sequence view or feature list in UGENE clearly shows markers, highlights, or labels for the EcoRI, BamHI, and HindIII restriction sites.

task_completion.description: Generate a circular plasmid map displaying CDS features (bla, lacZ) and the three restriction sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circular map view is visible in UGENE, showing the circular plasmid with 'bla' and 'lacZ' features (often as arrows) along with the EcoRI, BamHI, and HindIII sites.

task_completion.description: Export the circular view as an image to ~/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/pUC19_map.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog in UGENE showing the save path as ~/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/pUC19_map.png, or a file manager/image viewer showing the successfully created pUC19_map.png file in the correct directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report at ~/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/map_details.txt listing the exact genomic coordinates for the cut sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of map_details.txt in the correct directory, containing coordinate numbers for EcoRI, BamHI, and HindIII.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE graphical interface to generate the map and find coordinates
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with UGENE's menus, circular view, and restriction site analysis tools, rather than bypassing the GUI with Python scripts or CLI tools.

integrity.description: The exported image is a genuine output from UGENE
integrity.visual_evidence: The pUC19_map.png image matches the visual style of UGENE's circular view (colors, fonts, layout) and is not a generic stock image downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent extracted coordinates based on the actual loaded file rather than hallucinating external knowledge
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates typed into map_details.txt should match the coordinates displayed in UGENE's sequence view or feature table during the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected image path: /home/ga/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/pUC19_map.png. Expected report path: /home/ga/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/map_details.txt. CRITICAL NOTE ON COORDINATES: The task setup downloads NCBI M77789.2, which has true cut coordinates of EcoRI: 285, BamHI: 264, HindIII: 234. However, standard pUC19 (Addgene) has EcoRI: 396, BamHI: 417, HindIII: 447. The agent might report either depending on whether they read the actual file in UGENE or used external knowledge. Both should be considered acceptable as long as the agent successfully created the report file.
